seoTitle: How to Create Your First Jira Project Easily
metaDescription: A beginner's guide to creating your first Jira project, choosing a template, configuring settings, and inviting your team.
intro: Starting your first Jira project can feel overwhelming with all the template and setting options available. This guide breaks down creating a new project from scratch, picking the right template, and inviting your team so you can start tracking work right away.

steps.heading: Create a new project
steps.body: From your Jira dashboard, start the project creation flow to begin setting up your workspace.

steps.heading: Choose a project template
steps.body: Pick a template that fits your workflow, such as Scrum for sprint-based work or Kanban for continuous flow.

steps.heading: Configure project settings
steps.body: Set the project name, key, and any other core settings before finishing the setup.

steps.heading: Invite team members and finish setup
steps.body: Add your teammates to the project so everyone can start creating and working on issues together.

faq.question: How do I create my first Jira project?
faq.answer: Start the project creation flow from your dashboard, choose a template, configure the settings, and invite your team.

faq.question: Should I choose Scrum or Kanban for my first project?
faq.answer: Scrum fits teams working in fixed sprints, while Kanban works well for continuous, ongoing work without fixed iterations.

faq.question: Can I invite team members after creating the project?
faq.answer: Yes, you can invite team members during initial setup or add them later from the project's people settings.

faq.question: What's the difference between team-managed and company-managed projects?
faq.answer: Team-managed projects offer simpler, self-service configuration, while company-managed projects give administrators more centralized control.
